fact: Security remediation batch completed on 2026-02-27: Telegram token rotated, exposed credentials redacted from memory files, and ralphy execution now requires human approval plus PRD ownership validation.

Secondary hardening fixes completed on 2026-02-27: nightly-memory-extraction got symlink protection with atomic writes, audit-log input sanitization was added, and model switch scripts now use mktemp.

Morning Meeting security remediation (Mar 17, 11:52-12:30 ICT): 15 security findings addressed from nightly-security-review. HIGH priority: fixed secret logging redaction in webhook_decisions.jsonl (autonomous-trading-bot), removed ALLOW_NON_LOCALHOST_BIND override (autonomous-trading-bot), removed WEBHOOK_HOST override (futures-bot). MEDIUM priority: added startup warning for missing webhook secret, hardened trade_notifier.py to refuse world-readable config, added _ALLOWED_ENV_KEYS whitelist to both webhook receivers, documented client IP logging purpose, added fcntl locking + atomic writes to autonomy_gate.py, added X-Forwarded-For validation to futures webhook receiver, hardened brief_generator.py fallback path. LOW priority: several accepted-risk findings documented (plaintext .env secrets, no unified retention policy, shell env parsing, cron environment isolation).

Autonomy gate queue hardening (Mar 17, 12:23 ICT): scripts/autonomy_gate.py queue mode now uses fcntl.flock for queue lock file, switched queue writes to atomic temp-file replacement, and keeps read-heal-write cycle under one lock. Prevents race conditions across concurrent processes that could cause duplicate work or stale queue state.
